details.Architecture_Style: Sikh Architecture (Fusion of Islamic and Hindu styles)
details.Historical_Significance: The holiest site of Sikhism, it symbolizes human brotherhood and equality. It is famous for its 24-carat gold covering and the sacred Amrit Sarovar.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Continuous kirtan (devotional singing) and Langar (community kitchen).
details.Transport_Info: Well-connected by Amritsar Junction and Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: A sacred cave at 16,000 feet where a natural ice lingam forms annually.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Annual Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage.
details.Transport_Info: Base camps at Pahalgam or Baltal; requires a high-altitude trek.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas, located on the western bank of the Ganges.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Sapta Rishi Aarti and Ganga Snan.
details.Transport_Info: Connected via Varanasi Junction and Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Part of the Char Dham yatra, believed to have been built by the Pandavas and revived by Adi Shankara.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Darshan of the conical rock formation.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by a 16 km trek from Gaurikund.

details.Historical_Significance: Renowned for its 14 towering gopurams and the 'Hall of Thousand Pillars'. It is the spiritual heart of Madurai.
details.Managing_Authority: Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments (HR&CE) Dept.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Ritual bath in Potramarai Kulam (Golden Lotus tank).
details.Transport_Info: 2 km from Madurai Junction railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the most popular and richest temples in India. The idol is carved from a single black stone with the trunk tilted to the right.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Tuesday special Aartis and Sankashti Chaturthi pujas.
details.Transport_Info: Located at Prabhadevi, easily accessible via Dadar station.

details.Historical_Significance: Surrounded by 18 hills in the Periyar Tiger Reserve. One of the world's largest annual pilgrimage sites.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims observe 41 days of penance before climbing the 18 sacred steps.
details.Transport_Info: 93 km from Chengannur railhead; requires 5 km trek from Pamba.

details.Historical_Significance: Part of the Char Dham, famous for its annual Rath Yatra.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Mahaprasad distribution.
details.Transport_Info: Terminal railway station at Puri; 60 km from Bhubaneswar Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Built by Gopalrao Butti as the final resting place (Samadhi) of Sai Baba. It is a symbol of inter-religious harmony.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Kakad Aarti, Madhyan Aarti, and Shej Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Connected by Sainagar Shirdi station and Shirdi Airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Riches pilgrimage center in the world. Located atop Seshachalam Hills (Seven Hills).
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Suprabhatam and Laddu distribution as Prasadam.
details.Transport_Info: 22 km from Tirupati railway station; accessible by well-laid mountain road.

details.Historical_Significance: Pushkar is an ancient Adi-tirtha where Lord Brahma resides. It is considered the primary pilgrimage site, akin to how Madhusudan is the greatest among gods.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the three sacred Pushkar lakes (Jyeshtha, Madhya, and Kanishtha) and performing Shraddha at the Shuddhavapi (Gaya-kund).
details.Transport_Info: Motor-bus and tonga are available from Ajmer station, which is 7 miles away via a paved road.

details.Historical_Significance: Main center of Shuddhadvaita Pushtimarg established by Vallabhacharya. The idol was brought from Braj to protect it from invasions; the chariot wheels sank here, signaling the deity's wish to stay.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Seva-puja performed with darshan opening for short periods with matching adornments (shringar) and devotional singing.
details.Transport_Info: Located 7 miles west of Nathdwara station or accessible by motor-bus from Udaipur.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the seven main sub-peethas of the Vallabh Sampradaya. Believed that King Ambarish worshipped this exact idol.
details.Transport_Info: 11 miles from Nathdwara by motor route, and 9 miles from Nathdwara station.

details.Historical_Significance: Presiding deity of the Maharanas of Mewar, originally worshipped by Bappa Rawal. Houses a four-faced idol of Lord Shiva.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims must wear a special robe provided by the priests for darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Motor-bus available from Udaipur, which is 12 miles away.

details.Historical_Significance: Family deity of the Bikaner royal family; the state's foundation is attributed to her blessings.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims feed and avoid stepping on thousands of holy rats called 'Kaba'.
details.Transport_Info: Located on the Jodhpur-Bikaner railway line, next to Deshnoke station.

details.Historical_Significance: Barbarik, grandson of Bhima, was granted a boon by Krishna to be worshipped in Kaliyuga.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Jyeshtha Shukla 12, Kartik Shukla 12, and Phalguna Shukla 12
details.Transport_Info: 10 miles from Ringas station; accessible by foot or camel.

details.Historical_Significance: Features 1444 pillars and unparalleled architecture; built at a cost of 96 lakh rupees.
details.Transport_Info: 9 miles from Rani station on the Ahmedabad-Delhi line.

details.Historical_Significance: A Jain Atishaya Kshetra worshipped by Shwetambars, Digambars, and Bhils. Idol is offered so much saffron he is called Kesariyanathji.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Offering of large quantities of saffron (Kesar).
details.Transport_Info: Located 40 miles from Udaipur.

details.Historical_Significance: Prominent Atishaya Kshetra where a Padmasana idol was discovered underground near a river.
details.Transport_Info: 4 miles from Shri Mahavirji station on the Mathura-Nagda line.

details.Historical_Significance: Famous site where bones immersed in water dissolve in hours. Seven streams emerge from the mountain.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Chaitra Somvati Amavasya and Bhadrapad Amavasya
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Immersion of bones (Asthi-visarjan).
details.Transport_Info: 20 miles from Sikar or Nawalgarh; camels available.

details.Historical_Significance: Idol found while digging in the 11th century. Bhairavji is the presiding deity of this tirtha.
details.Transport_Info: Located 6 miles from Balotra station in the mountains.

details.Historical_Significance: Situated in a dark cave about 46 miles from Jammu; houses goddesses in 'Pindis' form.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims crawl through the cave for darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Bus from Jammu to Katra (31 miles), then trek.

details.Historical_Significance: On Alaknanda banks near Nar-Narayan mountains; one of the supreme Dhams.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Holy dip in Tapt Kund (hot spring) before darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Motor bus from Rishikesh/Joshimath.

details.Historical_Significance: Legendary forest where Brahma's Chakra fell; site where 88,000 sages did penance.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Holy dip in Chakra Tirtha and 84-Kosh Parikrama in Phalguna.
details.Transport_Info: Branch railway line from Balamau to Sitapur.

details.Historical_Significance: Birthplace of Maryada Purushottam Lord Rama on the banks of Sarayu river.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Ram Navami, Sawan Jhula, Kartik Purnima
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in Sarayu and Ayodhya Parikrama.
details.Transport_Info: Ayodhya station on Mughalsarai-Lucknow line.

details.Historical_Significance: Confluence of Ganga and Gandak; site of legendary battle between Gaj and Grah.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing at confluence during Kartik Purnima.
details.Transport_Info: Major station on Katihar-Gorakhpur railway line.

details.Historical_Significance: Near Brahmagiri mountain, origin of Godavari river brought by Sage Gautama.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Kumbh Mela (when Jupiter is in Leo)
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Dip in Kushavart Sarovar before darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by road, 18 miles from Nashik.

details.Historical_Significance: On Bhima river; Lord Vitthal stood on a brick for his devotee Pundalik.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims touch their heads to the Lord's feet.
details.Transport_Info: Connected via branch line from Kurduvadi station.

details.Historical_Significance: First of twelve Jyotirlingas; Moon God (Soma) worshipped here to cure a curse.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in Agni Kund and Prachi Triveni Sangam.
details.Transport_Info: 3 miles from Veraval station via paved road.

details.Historical_Significance: Important historical significance of Ayyappa Temple #1. [1]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bhajan, Archana [2]
details.Transport_Info: Bus, Train, Airport [2]

details.Historical_Significance: It is the main temple of Bhubaneswar (Ekamra Kshetra), boasting a massive spire. It represents a beautiful synthesis of Shaivism and Vaishnavism [3-6].
details.Managing_Authority: Archaeological Survey of India & Temple Trust
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the nearby Bindu Sagar lake before partaking in the darshan of Lord Lingaraj [8].
details.Transport_Info: Located around 18 miles from Cuttack, accessible easily from Bhubaneswar railway station on the Howrah-Waltair line [6].

details.Historical_Significance: Situated on the Nilachal Hill, it is one of the supreme 51 Shakti Peethas where Goddess Sati's reproductive organ (Yoni) fell. [9, 10]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Tantric worship rituals and bathing in the Brahmaputra river. [9, 10]
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via Amingaon railway station and Guwahati proper, crossing the Brahmaputra by steamer or bridge. [10]

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 sacred Jyotirlingas, situated near the banks of the holy Shipra river. Uniquely, the Jyotirlinga here faces south (Dakshinamurti) and sits below ground level. [12-14]
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Maha Shivaratri, Kumbh Mela (Simhastha every 12 years) [15, 16]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bhasma Aarti, where the deity is worshipped with fresh ashes every morning. [17]
details.Transport_Info: Ujjain is well-connected by major railway lines from Bhopal, Nagda, and Indore. [16, 17]

details.Historical_Significance: Located on the Mandhata island in the Narmada river, shaped like the sacred symbol 'Om'. It houses one of the 12 Jyotirlingas. [19-21]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Parikrama (circumambulation) of the Mandhata island and taking a holy dip in the Narmada river. [25, 26]
details.Transport_Info: Omkareshwar Road station is on the Ajmer-Khandwa line; from there, it is a 7-mile journey to the Narmada riverbank. [27, 28]

details.Build_Date: Rebuilt in 18th Century by Ahilyabai Holkar [29]
details.Historical_Significance: Renowned for a 40-centimeter-long footprint of Lord Vishnu stamped into solid rock, where he subdued the demon Gayasur. It is the premier site in India for ancestral offerings. [29-31]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Performing Pind Daan and Shraddha for ancestors, and bathing in the Falgu river. [29, 31]
details.Transport_Info: Gaya is a major junction on the Eastern Railway's main line. [33]

details.Historical_Significance: A rare site that is simultaneously one of the 12 Jyotirlingas and one of the 51 Shakti Peethas (where Sati's neck fell). Adi Shankaracharya and Shivaji Maharaj meditated here. [34, 35, 37]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the Patal Ganga (Krishna river) deep in the valley before worship. [39]
details.Transport_Info: The nearest major railway station is Kurnool; accessible via a steep forest motor-bus route. [39, 40]

details.Historical_Significance: Established by Lord Rama himself before bridging the sea to Lanka. It is a Jyotirlinga and features some of the longest and most spectacular temple corridors in the world. [41, 43, 45]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims ritually bathe in the 22 sacred Tirthas (wells) inside the temple grounds and the Agni Tirtha (sea) outside. [48, 49]
details.Transport_Info: Connected to the mainland by the famous Pamban Bridge on the Southern Railway line from Madras. [50, 51]

details.Historical_Significance: The temple marks the spot where Goddess Parvati incarnated as Princess Meenakshi and married Lord Shiva. Famous for its magnificent, towering Gopurams and the Thousand Pillared Hall. [52, 54]
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Chithirai Thiruvizha (Marriage of Meenakshi and Shiva) [54, 55]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the Swarna Pushkarini (Golden Lotus Tank) before having darshan. [52, 54]
details.Transport_Info: Madurai is a major junction on the Southern Railway line from Trichy to Tuticorin. [56]

details.Historical_Significance: Situated on the holy Venkatadri hill, it is one of the most visited and wealthiest pilgrimage centers globally. Lord Vishnu resides here to save mankind in Kali Yuga. [57, 59]
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees offer their hair (Mundan/Kalyanakatta) and make offerings in the sacred Hundi. [60]
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via the Renigunta or Tirupati East railway stations, followed by a bus journey up the Tirumala hills. [57, 58]

details.Historical_Significance: A highly revered shrine surrounded by Chinar trees. It is uniquely known for its sacred spring, whose water is believed to change color to indicate the future fate of the region.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Devotees predominantly offer milk and kheer (rice pudding) into the sacred spring.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible via motor bus or taxi from Srinagar, which is located about 14 miles away.

details.Build_Date: 19th Century (Golden dome added by Maharaja Ranjit Singh)
details.Historical_Significance: One of the 51 Maha Shakti Peethas where Sati's tongue is believed to have fallen. The temple has no idol; instead, natural eternal flames burning seamlessly from the rock crevices are worshipped.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Aarti is performed to the eternal flames. Offerings include rabri (sweet milk) and misri.
details.Transport_Info: Located 30 miles from Kangra. Accessible by road from Pathankot or the Jwalamukhi Road railway station.

details.Build_Date: Rebuilt in the 18th Century by Marathas
details.Historical_Significance: An ancient temple where the Pandavas, along with Lord Krishna, prayed to Lord Shiva before the Mahabharata war and received his blessings for victory. A few drops from its sacred sarovar reportedly cured King Vena's leprosy.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the holy Sarovar followed by Jalabhishek of the ancient Shivalinga.
details.Transport_Info: Easily accessible via Thanesar City Railway Station and local road transport.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed to be the incarnation of Yoga Maya who escaped Kansa and established herself in the Vindhya mountains. Forms the focal point of the famous 'Trikon Parikrama'.
details.Festivals_Celebrated: Chaitra Navratri, Ashwin Navratri, Kajali Mahotsav
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims perform the Trikon Parikrama covering Vindhyavasini, Ashtabhuja, and Kali Khoh shrines.
details.Transport_Info: Located 5 miles from Mirzapur. Vindhyachal is a prominent railway station on the Delhi-Howrah route.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas and also a Shakti Peetha where Sati's heart fell. The Jyotirlinga was brought by Ravana from Kailash but got permanently rooted here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Millions of 'Kanwariyas' fetch holy water from Sultanganj and trek to pour it over the Jyotirlinga during the month of Shravan.
details.Transport_Info: Located just 4 miles from Jasidih railway station on the Howrah-Patna main line.

details.Historical_Significance: Founded by the great Vaishnavite saint Shri Madhvacharya. The temple is unique globally because the Lord is worshipped only through a silver-plated window with nine holes, known as the Navagraha Kindi.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Elaborate daily offerings and chanting of Dwaita philosophy scriptures. Darshan is strictly through the Navagraha window.
details.Transport_Info: Located 37 miles from Mangalore; very well connected by the Konkan Railway and state buses.

details.Historical_Significance: Commissioned by the Hoysala King Vishnuvardhana. It is globally acclaimed for its exceptionally intricate soapstone carvings, star-shaped layout, and beautiful Madanika (celestial nymph) sculptures.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Agamic poojas dedicated to Lord Vishnu and his consorts.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 24 miles from Hassan Railway Station. Well connected by road from Bengaluru and Mysore.

details.Historical_Significance: A major Shakti Peetha where Goddess Sati's navel fell. It is famously known as Nabhi Gaya, making it an extremely prominent and holy site for performing ancestral rites (Shraddha).
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Pilgrims perform Pind Daan (ancestral rites) at the Nabhi Gaya well and bathe in the Vaitarani river.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 9 miles from the Jajpur Keonjhar Road railway station on the Howrah-Waltair line.

details.Build_Date: 10th - 14th Centuries (Expanded heavily by Chola and Pandya kings)
details.Historical_Significance: Foremost of the 108 Divya Desams (Swayam Vyakta Kshetra). Enclosed by seven concentric walls, it is the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world. Sri Ramanujacharya lived and taught here.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily worship follows the strict Pancharatra Agama. Devotees walk through the paramapada vaasal (heaven's gate) during Ekadashi.
details.Transport_Info: Srirangam is situated on an island just across the Cauvery river from Tiruchirappalli (Trichy), which has a major railway junction and airport.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Akasha (Aether/Space) element among the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas. It is famous for the 'Chidambara Rahasyam' (secret of Chidambaram) and is one of the rare temples where both Shiva and Vishnu are enshrined in close proximity.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Witnessing the Maha Aarti of the dancing Lord Nataraja and viewing the empty space symbolizing the Akasha Lingam.
details.Transport_Info: Chidambaram has its own railway station located on the main line between Chennai and Tiruchirappalli.

details.Historical_Significance: According to legend, Lord Krishna traveled here from Dwarka to honor the deep devotion of His aged devotee, Bodana. The temple houses the original idol brought from Dwarka.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Mangala Aarti, Rajbhog, and Shringara Darshan.
details.Transport_Info: Dakor is easily accessible by rail on the Anand-Godhra line and by state buses from Ahmedabad and Vadodara.

details.Historical_Significance: One of the 12 revered Jyotirlingas, situated in the ancient Dakini forest on the Sahyadri mountains. It is the origin of the Bhima river. Legend states Lord Shiva rested here after slaying the demon Tripurasura.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Rudrabhishek and Panchamrit Pooja performed daily.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible via a scenic, hilly road roughly 68 miles (110 km) from Pune.

details.Historical_Significance: The 12th Jyotirlinga, located extremely close to the famous Ellora Caves. The legend involves Lord Shiva resurrecting the dead son of his devout worshipper, Ghushma, and manifesting here eternally at her request.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Daily Jalabhishek and Shringar Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Located about 12 miles from Daulatabad and 18 miles from Aurangabad city. Well connected by road.

details.Historical_Significance: Houses the sacred 'Atmalinga' brought from Kailash by Ravana. Lord Ganesha deceitfully placed it on the ground, making it permanently rooted. It is revered on par with Kashi.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Darshan of the Atmalinga (only visible completely during the Ashtabandhana festival occurring once every 20 years).
details.Transport_Info: Gokarna Road railway station is on the Konkan Railway network, easily accessible by buses from Mangalore or Goa.

details.Historical_Significance: Believed to be the exact location where Lord Narasimha emerged from the pillar to slay Hiranyakashipu and save Prahlada. The surrounding hills house nine distinct shrines known as Nava Narasimha.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Thirumanjanam and specific Agamic daily Aradhanas.
details.Transport_Info: Accessible by road from Nandyal or Cuddapah; involves trekking to visit all nine hill shrines.

details.Historical_Significance: Legend says Lord Vishnu manifested here to save Prahlada from being thrown into the sea under a mountain. Uniquely, the deity is covered completely in sandalwood paste year-round, appearing like a Shiva Linga.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Nijaroopa Darshan (true form without sandalwood paste) is allowed only for 12 hours once a year on Akshaya Tritiya.
details.Transport_Info: Located on a hill about 10 miles from Visakhapatnam railway station, accessible by local buses and taxis.

details.Historical_Significance: An ancient Vaishnavite pilgrimage center on the banks of the Meshwo river. The temple walls contain exquisite stone carvings depicting scenes from the Ramayana and Mahabharata.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Bathing in the holy Meshwo river (Nagadhara) before the daily Aarti.
details.Transport_Info: Situated on the Delhi-Mumbai National Highway (NH-8), with excellent road connectivity.

details.Historical_Significance: Revered as one of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Goddess Tulja Bhavani is the Kuladevi (family deity) of the Maratha royal family, including Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, whom she is believed to have gifted a magical sword.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Chhakdi Puja and ceremonial Abhishekam of the Goddess.
details.Transport_Info: The nearest major railway station is Solapur, about 28 miles (45 km) away, connected by state transport buses.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Vayu (Air) element among the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas. Highly famous for the devotion of Kannappa Nayanar, the hunter who plucked out his own eyes to offer them to the bleeding Lingam.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: Rahu-Ketu Sarpadosha Nivarana Pooja. Priests do not touch the main Vayu Linga during worship.
details.Transport_Info: Located on the banks of the Swarnamukhi river, about 22 miles from Tirupati. Srikalahasti has its own railway station.

details.Historical_Significance: Represents the Appu (Water) element. The Shiva Linga constantly emits water from an underground spring. A legendary rivalry between a spider and an elephant worshipping the deity here resulted in both achieving salvation.
details.Rituals_and_Pujas: During Uchikala Pooja, the priest dresses in female attire to worship Lord Shiva, honoring Goddess Parvati's penance here.
details.Transport_Info: Located in Srirangam Island, just a short distance across the Cauvery river from Tiruchirappalli Railway Station.
